name: Joya de Nicaragua Antaño CT Toro
description: The Antaño CT pairs the bone-dry Nicaraguan Antaño blend with a silky Connecticut Broadleaf maduro wrapper for a rich, full-bodied smoke loaded with dark chocolate and leather. A standout value from the oldest cigar factory in Nicaragua.

name: Joya de Nicaragua Antaño CT Robusto
description: The shorter Robusto format of the Antaño CT concentrates the blend's bold Nicaraguan character under a dark Connecticut Broadleaf maduro wrapper, delivering an intense 50-minute smoke with dark chocolate and earthy pepper notes.

name: Joya de Nicaragua Antaño CT Gordo
description: The Gordo vitola of the Antaño CT offers the widest ring gauge in the line, providing a cooler, more relaxed burn that amplifies the blend's molasses and espresso sweetness against a dark, oily Connecticut Broadleaf maduro wrapper.

name: Joya de Nicaragua Uno Robusto
description: The Uno is Joya de Nicaragua's entry into approachable everyday cigars, featuring a smooth Ecuadorian Habano wrapper over a medium-bodied Nicaraguan blend that delivers cedar, nuts, and gentle spice at an accessible price point.

name: Joya de Nicaragua Numero Dos Robusto
description: Numero Dos builds on Joya's everyday line with a slightly fuller Nicaraguan filler profile that adds a touch of honey sweetness, all wrapped in a silky Ecuadorian Habano leaf for an accessible yet satisfying medium-bodied smoke.

name: Joya de Nicaragua Numero Tres Robusto
description: Numero Tres steps up the Estelí filler content for a noticeably more earthy and peppery finish compared to its siblings, while the golden Ecuadorian Habano wrapper keeps the smoke approachable for anyone seeking medium-strength Nicaraguan complexity.

name: Joya de Nicaragua Celebración No.2
description: The Celebración line honors the rich legacy of Joya de Nicaragua with an elegant Connecticut shade wrapper over a complex Nicaraguan blend, yielding a smooth, creamy smoke with hints of vanilla and white pepper for special occasions.

name: Joya de Nicaragua Cuatro Cinco Lancero
description: The Cuatro Cinco Lancero showcases the exceptional craftsmanship of Joya's rollers in the classic 7½ x 40 format, amplifying cedar and leather on the front palate before a long peppery finish — a favorite of connoisseurs who prefer narrow-gauge complexity.

name: Joya de Nicaragua Cinco Décadas Edmundo
description: Released to commemorate 50 years of the historic Joya de Nicaragua factory, the Cinco Décadas Edmundo is a powerhouse puro featuring an all-Nicaraguan construction and a deeply complex profile of dark chocolate, espresso, and oak with a long, bold finish.

name: Joya de Nicaragua Clásico No.1
description: The Clásico No.1 is Joya de Nicaragua's traditional mild-to-medium Connecticut shade offering, built for smokers who appreciate vintage-style accessible construction with creamy Jalapa tobaccos and a smooth, long draw.

name: Joya de Nicaragua Plasencia Joya Silver
description: A collaborative effort between Joya de Nicaragua and Plasencia Cigars, the Silver line combines a silky Ecuadorian Connecticut wrapper with a balanced blend of Nicaraguan and Honduran tobaccos for a smooth, creamy everyday smoke at an accessible price.

name: Joya de Nicaragua Antaño 1970 Corona Gorda
description: The Antaño 1970 Corona Gorda presents the legendarily full-bodied 1970 blend in a classic 5⅝ × 46 format, delivering the line's signature leather, black pepper, and dark chocolate character in a focused 55-minute smoke that exemplifies Nicaraguan puro power.

name: E.P. Carrillo Short Run 2019 Robusto
description: The Short Run 2019 is a small-batch annual release from Ernesto Perez-Carrillo showcasing a unique Ecuadorian Habano wrapper over aged Nicaraguan long-fillers, earning high praise for its complexity and a long cedary, chocolatey finish.

name: E.P. Carrillo Short Run 2020 Toro
description: The 2020 vintage of E.P. Carrillo's celebrated Short Run series adds Dominican filler to the Nicaraguan backbone for added complexity, wrapping everything in a rich Ecuadorian Habano leaf that delivers chocolate, cedar, and roasted notes across a long 60-minute smoke.

name: E.P. Carrillo Time For Fun Toro
description: Time For Fun is E.P. Carrillo's approachable Connecticut shade line, designed for lighter-smoking occasions with a creamy, smooth wrapper over a mild Nicaraguan core — a cigar Ernesto made specifically for those new to premium smokes.

name: E.P. Carrillo La Historia E-V
filler: Nicaraguan Jalapa, Estelí, Dominican San Andrés
description: The La Historia E-V is widely considered Ernesto Perez-Carrillo's masterwork, a complex medium-full Toro featuring an 8-year-aged Ecuadorian Habano wrapper over a blend of vintage Dominican and Nicaraguan tobaccos that delivers exceptional balance and long complexity.

name: E.P. Carrillo La Historia EH
filler: Nicaraguan Jalapa, Estelí, Dominican San Andrés
description: The La Historia EH Churchill is an extended expression of Carrillo's flagship blend, offering the same aged Dominican and Nicaraguan core as the E-V but in a longer format that coaxes out additional cedar and earthy complexity over a 65-minute smoke.

name: E.P. Carrillo Dusk Till Dawn Robusto
description: Dusk Till Dawn features a rich Mexican San Andrés maduro wrapper that gives the cigar its characteristic dark chocolate and coffee sweetness, paired with a medium-full Nicaraguan core that adds leather and earthy depth for a balanced after-dinner smoke.

name: E.P. Carrillo Selección Oscuro Toro
description: The Selección Oscuro Toro extends the maduro line's rich San Andrés wrapper profile into a longer format, intensifying the dark chocolate and coffee notes that made the Robusto a hit while adding cedar complexity across an extended 60-minute burn.

name: E.P. Carrillo INCH Maduro No.64
description: The INCH Maduro No.64 is the dark version of E.P. Carrillo's oversized INCH line, featuring a thick Connecticut Broadleaf maduro wrapper and a generous 64-ring gauge that produces a cool, creamy burn packed with dark chocolate and leather notes.

name: E.P. Carrillo New Wave Maduro Robusto
description: The New Wave Maduro Robusto offers a classic medium-full box-pressed construction wrapped in a dark Connecticut Broadleaf leaf, making it one of E.P. Carrillo's most popular everyday cigars for its balance of dark chocolate sweetness and Nicaraguan earthiness.

name: E.P. Carrillo Encantos Robusto
description: Encantos is E.P. Carrillo's Dominican-focused line featuring aged Cibao Valley tobaccos paired with a bright Ecuadorian Habano wrapper, producing a medium-bodied smoke with a honey-sweet entry and a creamy, nutty finish that is accessible yet sophisticated.

name: Camacho Liberty 2019
description: The 2019 Camacho Liberty is an annual limited release blended to celebrate American independence, featuring a rare Honduran Corojo wrapper and a complex tri-nation filler blend that delivers assertive cedar, leather, and black pepper with dark chocolate sweetness.

name: Camacho Liberty 2020 Toro
description: The 2020 Liberty continues Camacho's tradition of limited Fourth of July releases, featuring the same premium Honduran Corojo wrapper as the 2019 edition but with a slightly different filler ratio that emphasizes spice and earth over chocolate.

name: Camacho Diploma Robusto
description: The Camacho Diploma is a refined medium-body Honduran blend featuring an Ecuadorian Habano wrapper that softens the typical Camacho boldness, producing a nuanced robusto with leather, cedar, and toasted nuts for the smoker who wants approachability without sacrificing character.

name: Camacho Coyolar Robusto
description: The Coyolar is a Honduran puro from Camacho sourcing tobacco from the Coyolar Valley, a growing region adjacent to the prized Jamastran Valley, delivering a bold and spicy profile with pronounced leather and cedar over earthy Honduran terroir.

name: Camacho SP Habano Robusto
description: The SP Habano is a box-pressed Camacho designed for everyday enjoyment, featuring a smooth Ecuadorian Habano wrapper over a medium-full Honduran and Nicaraguan blend — a slightly softer entry point into the Camacho family without sacrificing its signature spice.

name: Camacho Corojo Cameroon Robusto
description: The Corojo Cameroon pairs Camacho's prized Honduran Jamastran Corojo filler with an African Cameroon wrapper, taming the typical Corojo boldness with the Cameroon leaf's subtle sweetness and woody complexity for a medium-full smoke with excellent balance.

name: Camacho Powerband Robusto
description: Powerband Robusto is the classic 5 × 50 expression of Camacho's bold Corojo-forward line, featuring a thick Honduran Corojo wrapper that delivers a consistent full-flavored ride of leather, cedar, and black pepper from start to finish.

name: Camacho Triple Maduro Robusto
description: Camacho's most celebrated maduro uses all three leaf positions fermented to maduro perfection — wrapper, binder, and filler — creating a deeply complex robusto loaded with dark chocolate, espresso, and molasses sweetness with a long, luscious finish.

name: Camacho American Barrel Aged Gordo
description: The American Barrel Aged Gordo showcases Camacho's unique process of aging filler tobaccos in American bourbon barrels, which imparts distinct vanilla and oak sweetness to the bold Honduran base blend, making this 60-ring gordo a uniquely American cigar experience.

name: Camacho Bravura Robusto
description: Bravura represents a more refined, medium-full expression of the Camacho brand using a tri-nation filler blend wrapped in an Ecuadorian Habano leaf, yielding a balanced smoke with notes of cedar, spice, and leather without the outright boldness of the Corojo line.

name: Camacho Liberty 2021
description: The 2021 Liberty edition continues Camacho's patriotic annual tradition with a limited-production Toro wrapped in a premium Honduran Corojo leaf, the result being a full-flavored, spice-forward smoke with earthy coffee undertones and a long peppery finish.

name: Camacho Ecuador Churchill
description: The Ecuador Churchill is Camacho's most mild offering, pairing an elegant Ecuadorian Connecticut shade wrapper with a smooth Honduran and Nicaraguan filler blend for a long, creamy smoke perfect for introductory cigar smokers or morning rituals.

name: Macanudo Café Lonsdale
description: The Macanudo Café Lonsdale is one of America's best-selling cigars in a classic elegant format, combining a silky Connecticut shade wrapper with aged Dominican and Jamaican tobaccos for a consistently mild, creamy smoke that has defined approachable cigar smoking for decades.

name: Macanudo Café Baron de Rothschild
description: The Baron de Rothschild is Macanudo Café's shorter, fatter petite corona format, offering the same classic Connecticut-Dominican character in a 45-minute smoke — ideal for those who love the Café blend but prefer a shorter, more convenient format.

name: Macanudo Café Hyde Park
description: The Hyde Park is one of Macanudo's most distinctive Café vitolas — a thick Churchill with a 49-ring gauge that burns slowly and coolly, amplifying the blend's creaminess and floral vanilla notes into a quintessential leisurely afternoon cigar.

name: Macanudo Inspirado Blue Robusto
description: The Inspirado Blue is Macanudo's medium-bodied Honduran-wrapped entry in the Inspirado series, offering a more assertive character than the classic Café line with notes of spice, cedar, and leather balanced by Dominican smoothness.

name: Macanudo Inspirado Red Robusto
description: Inspirado Red uses a Honduran Corojo wrapper to step up the spice and leather intensity in Macanudo's Inspirado line, delivering a medium-to-full body cigar with distinct Corojo character — a bolder departure from Macanudo's traditionally mild profile.

name: Macanudo Inspirado Green Robusto
description: Inspirado Green is the mild-medium expression of the Inspirado series, wrapping the Nicaraguan-Dominican blend in a silky Ecuadorian Connecticut leaf that produces the same creamy, smooth character Macanudo fans expect but with a slightly bolder Nicaraguan core.

name: Macanudo Vintage 1993 Churchill
description: The Vintage 1993 Churchill uses rare Dominican tobaccos aged since 1993 — nearly three decades of cellaring that transforms the classic Macanudo Café profile into an extraordinarily complex, silky smoke with oak, vanilla, and toasted nut depth far beyond any current production cigar.

name: Macanudo Estate Reserve Robusto
description: Macanudo Estate Reserve uses select reserve-grade Dominican tobaccos blended for extra aging potential, wrapping them in a flawless Ecuadorian Connecticut shade leaf for a premium Robusto that elevates the classic Café character with greater depth and a toasty, complex finish.

name: Macanudo Inspirado Tubo Robusto
description: The Inspirado Tubo Robusto is a convenient tube-packaged version of the Inspirado White, offering the same creamy Nicaraguan-Dominican blend under a silky Connecticut wrapper in individual aluminum tubes for freshness and portability.

name: Macanudo Inspirado Ecuador Robusto
description: Macanudo Inspirado Ecuador features a sun-grown Ecuadorian Habano wrapper that lends a natural sweetness and spice to the familiar Inspirado Nicaraguan-Dominican core, creating a medium-body smoke with a honey-spice character and a clean, cedar-laced finish.

name: Macanudo Café Lords
description: The Café Lords is a classic petite corona size in Macanudo's flagship mild line, offering the same beloved Connecticut-Dominican recipe in a lighter, shorter format that suits morning or post-lunch smoking perfectly.

name: Macanudo Café Claybourne
description: The Café Claybourne is Macanudo's smallest standard production vitola — a thin-ring petite corona that burns very cool and smooth, delivering the classic Café creaminess in a quick 40-minute smoke ideal for the morning commute or a short break.

name: Joya de Nicaragua Cinco Décadas Toro
description: The Cinco Décadas Toro is an alternate vitola of Joya de Nicaragua's legendary 50th-anniversary line, packing the same all-Nicaraguan full-bodied blend into a 6 × 52 format that burns for over an hour with extraordinary complexity — dark chocolate, espresso, and oak throughout.

name: E.P. Carrillo INCH No.66
description: The INCH No.66 is E.P. Carrillo's oversized flagship in the INCH line — a 66-ring gauge giant that delivers a remarkably cool, smooth burn showcasing cedar and leather throughout an extended 80-minute smoke for those who embrace the bigfoot format.

name: Camacho Powerband Gordo
description: The Powerband Gordo extends the bold Corojo-wrapped Honduran blend into a wider 60-ring gauge that tempers the spice into a slightly cooler, more coffee-forward profile while retaining the leather and black pepper backbone the Powerband line is known for.

name: Macanudo Café Prince of Wales
description: The Café Prince of Wales is Macanudo's longest standard Churchill format at 7½ inches, ideal for extended sessions that let the classic Connecticut-Dominican blend develop in waves of cream, vanilla, and subtle toastiness over a leisurely 70-minute smoke.

name: Joya de Nicaragua Antaño 1970 Robusto
description: The Antaño 1970 Robusto brings the intense, bone-dry full-body power of Joya's 1970 original recipe into a compact 5 × 50 format, delivering a concentrated blast of leather, earth, black pepper, and coffee that exemplifies why Joya de Nicaragua built its legendary reputation.

name: Camacho Triple Maduro Churchill
description: The Triple Maduro Churchill extends the beloved all-maduro construction into a classic 7 × 48 Churchill format, allowing the blend's dark chocolate, molasses, and espresso flavors to evolve through multiple distinct thirds across a lengthy and satisfying smoke.

name: E.P. Carrillo Pledge Sojourn Robusto
description: The Pledge Sojourn is an extension of E.P. Carrillo's critically acclaimed Pledge line, featuring the same Ecuadorian Habano wrapper and vintage Nicaraguan core with a Robusto-specific filler ratio that emphasizes cedar and leather with a spicy, peppery back end.

name: Macanudo Inspirado Nicaraguan Robusto
description: Macanudo Inspirado Nicaraguan is the brand's most Nicaragua-centric offering, wrapping an all-Nicaraguan filler blend in a natural Nicaraguan Habano wrapper for a medium-full body experience with earthy cedar and spice that departs significantly from the brand's mild Connecticut heritage.

name: Camacho Corojo Robusto Extra
description: The Corojo Robusto Extra adds a half inch to the classic Robusto format, giving the all-Honduran Corojo puro blend additional development time that softens the front-loaded spice and allows coffee and leather notes to emerge and linger on the extended finish.

name: Joya de Nicaragua Antaño Dark Corojo Toro
description: The Antaño Dark Corojo Toro uses an exceptionally dark Nicaraguan Corojo wrapper selected from the top of the plant for maximum oil content, pairing it with Joya's famously assertive Estelí and Jalapa filler blend for a powerful full-bodied smoke with great depth.

name: E.P. Carrillo Elencos Toro
description: Elencos represents a medium-bodied entry in E.P. Carrillo's portfolio, featuring an Ecuadorian sun-grown wrapper that adds natural sweetness and spice complexity to a well-aged Nicaraguan and Dominican filler blend for a versatile, everyday Toro with impressive balance.
